summary: Tested Data Preprocessing Pipeline UI and Advanced Hyperparameter Tuning UI features. Found and fixed critical bug in TuneView.js where tuning trials were returning 0.00% accuracy due to incorrect data access pattern. All features now working correctly. Backend API tests pass (10/10). Frontend features verified: Preprocess tab with 4 config cards, Tune tab with algorithm selection and tuning results, Deploy/What-If/PDF Report regression tests pass.

issues:

inside

in Badge component (cosmetic, doesn't affect functionality)

bugs_fixed.issue: Hyperparameter tuning showing 0.00% accuracy for all trials
bugs_fixed.root_cause: In TuneView.js, the code was accessing dataProfile.rows elements as arrays (r[i]) when they are actually objects with header keys. The correct access pattern is r[h] where h is the header name.
bugs_fixed.fix_applied: Changed `const v = r[i]` to `const v = r[h]` in both runTuning and applyBestParams functions in /app/frontend/src/components/views/TuneView.js
bugs_fixed.verification: After fix, tuning correctly shows Original: 80.83% → Tuned Best: 85.00% (+4.17% improvement)

critical_code_review_comments: Fixed critical bug in TuneView.js: dataProfile.rows contains objects (not arrays), so access should be r[h] not r[i]
Preprocess pipeline correctly integrates with training flow - preprocessConfig state is applied during handleTrain
Tune view correctly uses buildModelWithParams from mlEngine.js with HYPERPARAMETER_DEFS
All data-testid attributes properly implemented for testing

success_rate.frontend: 100% - All Preprocess, Tune, and regression tests passed (after bug fix)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Preprocess and Tune features are now fully working. Key fix: TuneView.js was accessing dataProfile.rows elements incorrectly (as arrays instead of objects). The fix changed r[i] to r[h] in both runTuning and applyBestParams functions. Tuning now correctly shows improvement (e.g., 80.83% → 85.00% for Random Forest). The Apply Tuned Model button only appears when improvement > 0. All existing features (Deploy, What-If, PDF Report) continue to work correctly.

rca_of_the_issue.bug: Hyperparameter tuning showing 0.00% accuracy for all trials
reproduction_steps: 2. Load a sample dataset (e.g., Loan Approval)
3. Select target column and train a model
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: In TuneView.js lines 90-97 and 150-157, the code was iterating over dataProfile.headers with index i and accessing dataProfile.rows[r][i]. However, dataProfile.rows contains objects with header keys (e.g., {Age: 25, Income: 50000}), not arrays. Accessing r[i] where i is a number returns undefined, causing all feature values to be undefined/NaN, which results in 0% accuracy for all model trials.
rca_of_the_issue.fix: Changed `const v = r[i]` to `const v = r[h]` in both runTuning (line 93) and applyBestParams (line 153) functions. This correctly accesses the row object by header key instead of numeric index.
rca_of_the_issue.verification: After fix, tuning correctly shows Original: 80.83% → Tuned Best: 85.00% (+4.17% improvement) with proper accuracy scores in the Top 10 Trials table.
